ctxMarket: Attention is centered on the major exploit of Drift Protocol, with estimates of losses ranging from $200M to $285M and operations paused. Institutional focus is also shifting as B2C2 designates Solana as its primary stablecoin network. Solana’s transaction count surpassed 10 billion, highlighting ongoing network activity.
ctxTalking:
• Drift Protocol’s suspected $200M+ exploit and operational halt
• B2C2’s move to route institutional stablecoin transactions on Solana
• GalaxyOne launching zero-fee Solana staking with a 6.5% yield
ctxMatters: Drift’s exploit raises concerns about security and trust in Solana DeFi platforms. B2C2’s adoption signals growing institutional confidence in Solana’s infrastructure for stablecoin settlement. High transaction volumes and new staking options suggest continued user and developer engagement despite recent setbacks.
ctxSignal:
Signal: The Drift Protocol exploit and B2C2’s Solana integration reflect real shifts in risk and institutional adoption.
Noise: Speculation about short-term sell pressure from Alameda’s Ethereum moves lacks direct evidence for Solana impact.
ctxGlossary:
Drift Protocol — A Solana-based decentralized trading platform
B2C2 — An institutional liquidity provider now routing stablecoin settlements on Solana

readMore.title: Drift Protocol halts operations after suspected $285m exploit as funds move across wallets
Drift Protocol warns users to pause deposits amid 'unusual' trading activity
